title: Fremya lucens
intro:
There is no information on a plant called "Fremya lucens." However, I found a plant called "Fremontodendron californicum" also known as Fremont's flannel bush and another plant called "Fremontodendron californicum's relative" Fremontia californica is not the same plant but Fremya is possibly a misspelling of Fremontia,  the plant Fremontia californica, also known as Fremontia or California flannelbush is a shrub native to California. 

However, if we assume that the plant you are referring to is indeed Fremontia, here's a paragraph about it:

Fremontia californica, also known as Fremontia or California flannelbush, is a shrub native to California, characterized by its greyish-brown bark, yellow flowers, and greyish-green leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations and promote wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Fremontia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Fremontia is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Fremya lucens, also known as the golden saxifrage, is a flowering plant species classified within the family Saxifragaceae. This family comprises approximately 500 species of flowering plants, often found in moist environments. As a member of Saxifragaceae, Fremya lucens is a part of the larger group of flowering plants known as angiosperms. The Saxifragaceae family is further classified within the order Saxifragales, which includes other plant families such as the Cercidiphyllaceae and the Crassulaceae. The classification of Fremya lucens reflects its evolutionary relationships with other flowering plants.
